title: Dynamic Programming Explained by Peter and Stewie!

dialogue.line: Stewie, what's this 'dynamic programming' everyone talks about?
Peter, think of it like meal prepping for your brain.
What? Are we feeding my brain chicken and rice?
No, it's storing solutions to solve bigger problems faster.
Dynamic programming breaks complex problems into simpler subproblems.
It calculates solutions once, avoiding redundant calculations.
So, like saving time on leftovers? Makes sense!
Exactly, it's used in algorithms like the Fibonacci sequence.
More time for TV! I love this dynamic programming.
You'll be the Einstein of couch potatoes, Peter!
